# Implementation Checklist: `url_fetch` Built-in Tool

**Spec:** `docs/plans/025_url_fetch_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/7
**Created:** 2026-03-05

---

## Phase 1: Toolname Constant & Validation (no dependencies)

- [ ] Add `URLFetch = "url_fetch"` constant to `internal/toolname/toolname.go` const block (Req 1.1)
- [ ] Add `URLFetch: true` entry to the `ValidNames()` map in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` (Req 1.2)
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: change expected count from 5 to 6 (Req 5.1)
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ContainsAllExpectedNames` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add `URLFetch` to the `expected` slice (Req 5.2)
- [ ] Update `TestConstants_Values` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add table entry `{"URLFetch", URLFetch, "url_fetch"}` (Req 5.3)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify `internal/toolname` tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 2: Tool Tests (RED phase — write failing tests first)

- [ ] Create `internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go` with `package tool` declaration
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Success` — httptest server returns `"hello world"` status 200; verify `IsError` false, `Content` equals `"hello world"`, `CallID` matches (Req 3.12, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_EmptyURL` — empty `Arguments` map `{}`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"url is required"` (Req 3.3, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_MissingURLArgument` — `Arguments: {"url": ""}`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"url is required"` (Req 3.3, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_UnsupportedScheme_File` — `"file:///etc/passwd"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` and `"file"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_UnsupportedScheme_FTP` — `"ftp://example.com/file"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` and `"ftp"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_NoScheme` — `"example.com"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxStatus_404` — httptest server returns `"not found"` status 404; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 404"` and `"not found"` (Req 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxStatus_500` — httptest server returns `"internal error"` status 500; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 500"` and `"internal error"` (Req 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_LargeResponseTruncation` — httptest server returns 20000 bytes of `'A'`; verify `IsError` false, `Content` contains `"[response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"`, `len(Content)` > 10000 but < 20000 (Req 3.10, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ExactLimitNotTruncated` — httptest server returns exactly 10000 bytes; verify `IsError` false, `Content` does NOT contain `"truncated"`, `len(Content)` equals 10000 (Req 3.10, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ContextCancellation` — httptest server blocks with `time.Sleep(10s)`, context timeout 50ms; verify `IsError` true (Req 3.7, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ConnectionRefused` — URL to closed port on 127.0.0.1; verify `IsError` true, `Content` non-empty (Req 3.7, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_CallIDPassthrough` — httptest server returns 200, call.ID `"uf-unique-42"`; verify `CallID` equals `"uf-unique-42"` (Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_EmptyResponseBody` — httptest server returns 200 with empty body; verify `IsError` false, `Content` equals `""` (Req 3.12, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxWithLargeBody` — httptest server returns 500 with > 10000 bytes; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 500"` and `"[response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` (Req 3.10, 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Confirm all 15 tests compile but fail (RED state — `urlFetchEntry` does not exist yet)

## Phase 3: Tool Implementation (GREEN phase)

- [ ] Create `internal/tool/url_fetch.go` with `package tool` declaration (Req 2.1)
- [ ] Define package-level constant `maxReadBytes = 10000` (Req 3.9, Constraint 7)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchEntry() ToolEntry` returning `ToolEntry{Definition: urlFetchDefinition, Execute: urlFetchExecute}` (Req 2.2)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchDefinition() provider.Tool` with `Name: toolname.URLFetch`, description, and single `url` parameter (Type `"string"`, Required `true`) (Req 2.3, 2.4)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchExecute(ctx, call, ec) provider.ToolResult` with deferred `toolVerboseLog` (Req 3.1, 3.13):
  - [ ] Extract `url` from `call.Arguments["url"]`; return error if empty (Req 3.2, 3.3)
  - [ ] Parse URL via `net/url.Parse`; return error on failure (Req 3.4)
  - [ ] Validate scheme is `"http"` or `"https"`; return `unsupported scheme %q: only http and https are allowed` on failure (Req 3.5)
  - [ ] Create request via `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", urlStr, nil)`; return error on failure (Req 3.6)
  - [ ] Execute via `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`; return `err.Error()` on failure (Req 3.7)
  - [ ] Defer `resp.Body.Close()` (Req 3.8)
  - [ ] Read body via `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1))`; return error on failure (Req 3.9)
  - [ ] Truncate if `len(body) > maxReadBytes`: slice to `maxReadBytes`, append `"\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` (Req 3.10)
  - [ ] If status < 200 or >= 300: return `ToolResult{IsError: true}` with `"HTTP %d: %s"` format (Req 3.11)
  - [ ] If status 200-299: return `ToolResult{IsError: false}` with body string (Req 3.12)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify all 15 `url_fetch_test.go` tests pass (GREEN state)

## Phase 4: Registry Registration

- [ ] Add `r.Register(toolname.URLFetch, urlFetchEntry())` to `RegisterAll()` in `internal/tool/registry.go` (Req 4.1)
- [ ] Add `TestRegisterAll_RegistersURLFetch` to `internal/tool/registry_test.go`: verify `r.Has(toolname.URLFetch)` after `RegisterAll` (Spec 7.4)
- [ ] Add `TestRegisterAll_ResolvesURLFetch` to `internal/tool/registry_test.go`: verify resolved tool has `Name == toolname.URLFetch` and `url` parameter (Spec 7.4)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify registry tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 5: Agent Scaffold Update

- [ ] Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go`: change tools comment from `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command` to `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch` (Req 6.1)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify `internal/agent` tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 6: Full Validation

- [ ] Run `make test` — all tests pass with zero failures

# Implementation Instructions

1. Study the spec markdown file thoroughly
    - located in `./docs/plans/`
    - Use the spec file that starts with 025
    - If 025 is empty, blank, or does not exist; the newest file formatted as `xxx_topic_spec.md`
2. Study implementation plan markdown file thoroughly
    - located in `./docs/plans/`
    - Use the spec file that starts with 025
    - If 025 is empty, blank, or does not exist; the newest file formatted as `xxx_topic_implement.md`
3. Pick the highest leverage unchecked task
4. Complete the task
5. Verify the changes against `## Rules and Checks` listed below.
5. Update the implementation plan to check off the task you just completed
6. After completeing a single phase, run the `auto-review` skill.
7, After the review changes are finished, run `git diff --shortstat` to check the total line changes.
    - If the changes are greater than 500 stop implementing and let the human know.
    - If the changes are less than 500 continue to the next phase of the implementation plan
8. Repeat steps 3-7 until all phases are complete.

## Rules

- Use agents for exploring the codebase
    - Each agent must only have one explicit task.
    - Each agent must only return the information that fulfills that task.
    - Spin off multiple agents in parallel for tasks that do not depend on another task.
- Use red/greed TDD
- If there are any SQL/ORM changes, please compare them against working queries in the codebase for syntax correctness.
- Verify that the unit tests for functions with updates have a test case to that will verify the change.
- Verify that all new code has an unbiased unit test.
- Verify that no import paths were changed for files in the `/lambdas` directory.
    - If tests require different import resolution than production, fix the test infrastructure (conftest.py, pytest.ini) without modifying the Lambda source imports.
- If there was an attempt at fixing a pattern, you must make a comprehensive search proving ALL instances are addressed.

<content>1: # Specification: `url_fetch` Built-in Tool
2: 
3: **Status:** Draft
4: **Version:** 1.0
5: **Created:** 2026-03-05
6: **GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/7
7: **Scope:** HTTP GET tool with response truncation, scheme validation, and graceful error handling
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## 1. Purpose
12: 
13: Implement the `url_fetch` tool — a built-in tool registered in the `Registry` that performs an HTTP GET request and returns the response body as text. This tool is needed for feature parity when axe replaces the custom container worker in Agent Engine.
14: 
15: This tool follows the same pattern as the M3-M7 tools (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`):
16: 
17: - **`RegisterAll`** — extended with one additional `r.Register(...)` call
18: - **`toolname.URLFetch`** — new constant declared in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`
19: - **`ExecContext`** — reused for `Stderr` and `Verbose` (verbose logging). `Workdir` is not used by this tool.
20: 
21: This tool does NOT use `validatePath` or `isWithinDir`. It operates on URLs, not file paths. There is no filesystem interaction.
22: 
23: ---
24: 
25: ## 2. Design Decisions
26: 
27: The following decisions were made during planning and are binding for implementation:
28: 
29: 1. **HTTP GET only.** The tool performs a single HTTP GET request. No POST, PUT, DELETE, or other methods. No request body. No custom headers.
30: 
31: 2. **Scheme validation.** Only `http://` and `https://` schemes are accepted. Any other scheme (e.g., `file://`, `ftp://`, `data:`) is rejected before any network request is made. This prevents the LLM from reading local files or using unexpected protocols.
32: 
33: 3. **URL parsing via `net/url.Parse`.** The `url` argument is parsed using Go's `net/url.Parse()`. If parsing fails, the tool returns an error. The parsed URL's `Scheme` field is checked against `"http"` and `"https"`.
34: 
35: 4. **Context-based timeout.** The tool creates an `http.Request` using `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", url, nil)`. The `ctx` parameter from the executor signature is passed directly. This means the tool inherits whatever timeout the caller provides (from the agent's `--timeout` flag). The tool does not impose its own timeout.
36: 
37: 5. **`http.DefaultClient` for execution.** The tool uses `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)` to execute the request. No custom transport, no TLS configuration, no cookie jar. `http.DefaultClient` follows redirects (up to 10 by default per Go stdlib).
38: 
39: 6. **Response body read limit via `io.LimitReader`.** The response body is read using `io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1)` where `maxReadBytes` is 10000. Reading one byte beyond the limit allows detecting whether truncation occurred. If exactly `maxReadBytes+1` bytes are read, the response is truncated to `maxReadBytes` bytes and a truncation notice is appended.
40: 
41: 7. **Truncation notice.** When the response exceeds 10000 bytes, the content is truncated to 10000 bytes and the string `"\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` is appended. Truncation is byte-level, not rune-aware.
42: 
43: 8. **Non-2xx status codes return an error.** If the HTTP response status code is outside the 200-299 range, the tool returns `ToolResult{IsError: true}` with content in the format `"HTTP %d: %s"` where the first value is the status code and the second value is the response body (truncated if needed). The response body is still read and included because it often contains useful error information.
44: 
45: 9. **Success result.** For 2xx responses, the tool returns `ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: string(body), IsError: false}` where `body` is the response body after truncation if applicable.
46: 
47: 10. **`resp.Body.Close()` via `defer`.** The response body is closed via `defer resp.Body.Close()` immediately after a successful `http.DefaultClient.Do()` call.
48: 
49: 11. **Network and DNS errors.** If `http.DefaultClient.Do()` returns an error (DNS failure, connection refused, TLS error, context cancellation), the tool returns `ToolResult{CallID: call.ID, Content: err.Error(), IsError: true}`.
50: 
51: 12. **Single parameter: `url`.** The tool has one required parameter named `url` of type `string`. There are no optional parameters. An empty or missing `url` is rejected before any parsing or network request.
52: 
53: 13. **`call_agent` remains outside the registry.** Same as all prior tool milestones.
54: 
55: 14. **No new external dependencies.** Uses only Go stdlib (`net/http`, `net/url`, `io`, `fmt`, `context`).
56: 
57: ---
58: 
59: ## 3. Requirements
60: 
61: ### 3.1 Tool Name Constant
62: 
63: **Requirement 1.1:** Add a new constant `URLFetch = "url_fetch"` to the `const` block in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`.
64: 
65: **Requirement 1.2:** Add `URLFetch: true` to the map returned by `ValidNames()` in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`.
66: 
67: ### 3.2 `url_fetch` Tool Definition
68: 
69: **Requirement 2.1:** Create a file `internal/tool/url_fetch.go`.
70: 
71: **Requirement 2.2:** Define an unexported function `urlFetchEntry() ToolEntry` that returns a `ToolEntry` with both `Definition` and `Execute` set to non-nil functions.
72: 
73: **Requirement 2.3:** The tool definition returned by the `Definition` function must have:
74: - `Name`: the value of `toolname.URLFetch` (i.e., `"url_fetch"`)
75: - `Description`: a clear description for the LLM explaining the tool fetches content from a URL via HTTP GET and returns the response body as text
76: - `Parameters`: one parameter:
77:   - `url`:
78:     - `Type`: `"string"`
79:     - `Description`: a description stating it is the URL to fetch
80:     - `Required`: `true`
81: 
82: **Requirement 2.4:** The tool name in the definition must use `toolname.URLFetch`, not a hardcoded string.
83: 
84: **Requirement 2.5:** `ToolCall.Arguments` is `map[string]string`. The `url` parameter is a string used directly — no type conversion is needed.
85: 
86: ### 3.3 `url_fetch` Tool Executor
87: 
88: **Requirement 3.1:** The `Execute` function must have the signature: `func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult`.
89: 
90: **Requirement 3.2:** Extract the `url` argument from `call.Arguments["url"]`.
91: 
92: **Requirement 3.3 (Empty URL Check):** If `url` is an empty string (including when the key is absent from the map), return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "url is required"`, `IsError: true`.
93: 
94: **Requirement 3.4 (URL Parsing):** Parse the URL using `net/url.Parse(urlStr)`. If parsing fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content` containing the parse error message, `IsError: true`.
95: 
96: **Requirement 3.5 (Scheme Validation):** After successful parsing, check that the URL's `Scheme` field is either `"http"` or `"https"`. If not, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: "unsupported scheme %q: only http and https are allowed"` (where `%q` is the actual scheme), `IsError: true`. If the scheme is empty (URL has no scheme), the error message must reflect this (the empty string formatted with `%q` produces `""`).
97: 
98: **Requirement 3.6 (Create HTTP Request):** Create an HTTP request using `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", urlStr, nil)`. If request creation fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
99: 
100: **Requirement 3.7 (Execute Request):** Execute the request using `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`. If execution fails (network error, DNS failure, TLS error, context cancellation), return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
101: 
102: **Requirement 3.8 (Close Response Body):** Immediately after a successful `Do()` call, defer `resp.Body.Close()`.
103: 
104: **Requirement 3.9 (Read Response Body):** Read the response body using `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1))` where `maxReadBytes` is 10000 (a package-level constant). If reading fails, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: err.Error()`, `IsError: true`.
105: 
106: **Requirement 3.10 (Truncation):** If the length of the read byte slice exceeds `maxReadBytes` (i.e., `len(body) > maxReadBytes`), truncate the slice to `maxReadBytes` bytes and convert to string. Append `"\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` to the string.
107: 
108: **Requirement 3.11 (Non-2xx Status):** If `resp.StatusCode < 200 || resp.StatusCode >= 300`, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content` in the format `"HTTP %d: %s"` (where the first value is `resp.StatusCode` and the second value is the body string after truncation if applicable), `IsError: true`.
109: 
110: **Requirement 3.12 (Success Case):** If the status code is in the 200-299 range, return a `ToolResult` with `CallID: call.ID`, `Content: bodyStr` (after truncation if applicable), `IsError: false`.
111: 
112: **Requirement 3.13 (Verbose Logging):** Use `toolVerboseLog(ec, toolname.URLFetch, result, summary)` via a deferred function, consistent with all other tools. The summary should include the URL (or a truncated version for long URLs) and the HTTP status code when available.

170: ## 5. Edge Cases
171: 
172: ### 5.1 URL Argument
173: 
174: | Scenario | Input `url` | Behavior |
175: |----------|------------|----------|
176: | Empty URL | `""` | Error: `"url is required"` |
177: | Missing `url` key in Arguments map | Key absent | `call.Arguments["url"]` returns `""` -> Error: `"url is required"` |
178: | Valid HTTPS URL | `"https://example.com"` | Normal HTTP GET. Returns response body. |
179: | Valid HTTP URL | `"http://example.com"` | Normal HTTP GET. Returns response body. |
180: | URL without scheme | `"example.com"` | `net/url.Parse` succeeds but `Scheme` is empty. Error: `unsupported scheme "": only http and https are allowed`. |
181: | File scheme | `"file:///etc/passwd"` | Error: `unsupported scheme "file": only http and https are allowed`. |
182: | FTP scheme | `"ftp://files.example.com/data"` | Error: `unsupported scheme "ftp": only http and https are allowed`. |
183: | Data URI | `"data:text/plain;base64,SGVsbG8="` | Error: `unsupported scheme "data": only http and https are allowed`. |
184: | Malformed URL | `"://bad"` | `net/url.Parse` returns an error. Error includes parse error message. |
185: | URL with port | `"http://localhost:8080/path"` | Normal HTTP GET. Port is part of the URL. |
186: | URL with query string | `"https://api.example.com/data?key=val"` | Normal HTTP GET. Query string is preserved. |
187: | URL with fragment | `"https://example.com/page#section"` | Normal HTTP GET. Fragment is not sent to server (per HTTP spec). |
188: | URL with auth | `"https://user:pass@example.com"` | Normal HTTP GET. Go's http client includes auth in the request. |
189: | Very long URL | 10,000+ character URL | Passed to http client. Server may reject with 414. Tool reports the server's response. |
190: 
191: ### 5.2 HTTP Status Codes
192: 
193: | Scenario | Status Code | Behavior |
194: |----------|------------|----------|
195: | 200 OK | 200 | `IsError: false`. Content is response body. |
196: | 201 Created | 201 | `IsError: false`. Content is response body. (Any 2xx is success.) |
197: | 204 No Content | 204 | `IsError: false`. Content is empty string (no body). |
198: | 301 Moved Permanently | 301 | `http.DefaultClient` follows the redirect (up to 10 hops). Final response determines success/error. |
199: | 302 Found | 302 | Same as 301 — redirect is followed automatically. |
200: | 400 Bad Request | 400 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 400: <body>"`. |
201: | 401 Unauthorized | 401 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 401: <body>"`. |
202: | 403 Forbidden | 403 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 403: <body>"`. |
203: | 404 Not Found | 404 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 404: <body>"`. |
204: | 429 Too Many Requests | 429 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 429: <body>"`. |
205: | 500 Internal Server Error | 500 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 500: <body>"`. |
206: | 502 Bad Gateway | 502 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 502: <body>"`. |
207: | 503 Service Unavailable | 503 | `IsError: true`. Content: `"HTTP 503: <body>"`. |
208: 
209: ### 5.3 Response Body Truncation
210: 
211: | Scenario | Response size | Behavior |
212: |----------|--------------|----------|
213: | Response < 10000 bytes | < 10000 bytes | No truncation. Content is full body. |
214: | Response = 10000 bytes exactly | 10000 bytes | No truncation. The threshold is "exceeds", not "equals". (`io.LimitReader` reads 10001 max; if read returns exactly 10000 bytes, no truncation.) |
215: | Response > 10000 bytes | > 10000 bytes | Truncated to 10000 bytes. `"\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` appended. |
216: | Large response on success (2xx) | > 10000 bytes, status 200 | Content: first 10000 bytes + truncation notice. `IsError: false`. |
217: | Large response on error (non-2xx) | > 10000 bytes, status 500 | Content: `"HTTP 500: "` + first 10000 bytes + truncation notice. `IsError: true`. |
218: | Truncation at multi-byte boundary | Truncated mid-UTF-8 sequence | Truncation occurs at the byte level. The resulting string may contain an incomplete UTF-8 sequence. No rune-aware truncation is performed. |
219: | Empty response body | 0 bytes | Content is empty string (or `"HTTP <code>: "` for non-2xx). |
220: | Binary response | Binary content | Content contains raw bytes as string. No encoding detection or conversion. |
221: 
222: ### 5.4 Network Errors
223: 
224: | Scenario | Behavior |
225: |----------|----------|
226: | DNS resolution failure | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message (e.g., `"dial tcp: lookup nonexistent.invalid: no such host"`). |
227: | Connection refused | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
228: | Connection timeout | `Do()` returns an error (via context deadline). `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
229: | TLS certificate error | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message (e.g., `"tls: certificate is not trusted"`). |
230: | Context cancelled before request | `Do()` returns context error immediately. `IsError: true`. Content: `"context canceled"` or similar. |
231: | Context cancelled during response read | `io.ReadAll` returns a context error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
232: | Server closes connection mid-response | `io.ReadAll` returns an error (e.g., `"unexpected EOF"`). `IsError: true`. Content: error message. |
233: | Redirect loop (> 10 hops) | `Do()` returns an error. `IsError: true`. Content: error message about redirect loop. |
234: 
235: ### 5.5 Argument Handling
236: 
237: | Scenario | Behavior |
238: |----------|----------|
239: | `url` argument present | Normal operation. |
240: | `url` argument missing from `call.Arguments` | `call.Arguments["url"]` returns `""` -> Error: `"url is required"`. |
241: | `url` argument is empty string | Error: `"url is required"`. |
242: | Whitespace-only URL | Passed to `net/url.Parse`. Parsing may succeed or fail depending on content. If scheme validation fails, appropriate error. Not special-cased — the tool does not trim whitespace. |

The only modifications outside the new files are: one line added to `RegisterAll` in `registry.go`, constant and `ValidNames` update in `toolname.go`, test updates in `toolname_test.go`, scaffold comment in `agent.go`, and new registration tests in `registry_test.go`.

392: ## 9. Out of Scope
393: 
394: The following items are explicitly **not** included in this milestone:
395: 
396: 1. HTTP methods other than GET (POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)
397: 2. Custom request headers (User-Agent, Authorization, Accept, etc.)
398: 3. Request body / payload
399: 4. Response format conversion (HTML-to-text, JSON formatting, Markdown conversion)
400: 5. Response encoding detection or conversion (charset handling)
401: 6. Custom TLS configuration or certificate pinning
402: 7. Retry logic or exponential backoff
403: 8. Rate limiting or request throttling
404: 9. Caching of responses
405: 10. Cookie handling beyond `http.DefaultClient` defaults
406: 11. Proxy configuration beyond `http.DefaultClient` defaults (which respects `HTTP_PROXY` env)
407: 12. Custom timeout per tool invocation (inherits caller's context)
408: 13. Configurable truncation limit (hardcoded at 10000 bytes)
409: 14. Streaming response output
410: 15. Following `meta` refresh redirects (only HTTP 3xx redirects are followed)
411: 16. Robot.txt compliance

417: ## 10. References
418: 
419: - GitHub Issue: https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/7

421: - `RegisterAll` function: `internal/tool/registry.go:92`
422: - `ValidNames` function: `internal/toolname/toolname.go:18`
423: - `provider.Tool` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:34`
424: - `provider.ToolCall` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:41`
425: - `provider.ToolResult` type: `internal/provider/provider.go:48`
426: - `ToolEntry` type: `internal/tool/registry.go:20`
427: - `ExecContext` type: `internal/tool/registry.go:13`
428: - `toolVerboseLog` helper: `internal/tool/verbose.go:11`
429: - `Scaffold` function: `internal/agent/agent.go:157`
430: - Agent config `tools` field: `internal/agent/agent.go:45`
431: - Tool call milestones: `docs/plans/000_tool_call_milestones.md`
432: 
433: ---
434: 
435: ## 11. Notes
436: 
437: - **No path validation needed.** Unlike file tools, `url_fetch` operates on URLs, not file paths. The scheme validation (`http`/`https` only) is the security boundary, preventing `file://` access to the local filesystem.
438: - **`io.LimitReader` for memory safety.** Without a read limit, the LLM could instruct the tool to fetch a multi-gigabyte resource, causing OOM. `io.LimitReader` caps memory usage at `maxReadBytes+1` bytes regardless of response size. The extra byte is used to detect truncation without requiring a second read or `Content-Length` header inspection.
439: - **`http.DefaultClient` follows redirects.** Go's default client follows up to 10 redirects. This is desirable behavior — the LLM doesn't need to handle redirects manually. If a redirect loop occurs, `Do()` returns an error which the tool reports.
440: - **Byte-level truncation, not rune-aware.** Consistent with `run_command` (100KB byte truncation). For web content (predominantly ASCII/UTF-8), byte-level truncation rarely breaks in practice. The truncation notice clearly indicates truncation occurred.
441: - **Non-2xx body is included in error.** Many APIs return useful error information in the response body (JSON error objects, HTML error pages). Including the body (truncated) in the error result gives the LLM actionable information.
442: - **No `Workdir` usage.** Unlike file tools, `url_fetch` does not use `ExecContext.Workdir`. The tool operates on network URLs, not filesystem paths. `Workdir` is irrelevant.
443: - **The `RegisterAll` pattern continues.** This adds the sixth tool: `list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`, `url_fetch`.
444: - **Security model.** The `url_fetch` tool allows the LLM to make arbitrary HTTP GET requests to any `http://` or `https://` URL. This is by design — if an agent has `tools = ["url_fetch"]` in its TOML config, the user has explicitly opted in. Scheme validation prevents `file://` abuse. Network-level restrictions (firewalls, egress policies) are outside axe's scope.

(End of file - total 444 lines)

# Specification: `url_fetch` Built-in Tool

**Status:** Draft
**Version:** 1.0
**Created:** 2026-03-05
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/7
**Scope:** HTTP GET tool with response truncation, scheme validation, and graceful error handling

---

## 1. Purpose

Implement the `url_fetch` tool — a built-in tool registered in the `Registry` that performs an HTTP GET request and returns the response body as text. This tool is needed for feature parity when axe replaces the custom container worker in Agent Engine.

This tool follows the same pattern as the M3-M7 tools (`list_directory`, `read_file`, `write_file`, `edit_file`, `run_command`):

- **`RegisterAll`** — extended with one additional `r.Register(...)` call
- **`toolname.URLFetch`** — new constant declared in `internal/toolname/toolname.go`
- **`ExecContext`** — reused for `Stderr` and `Verbose` (verbose logging). `Workdir` is not used by this tool.

12: // ExecContext holds the minimal context needed by generic tool executors.
13: type ExecContext struct {
14: 	Workdir string
15: 	Stderr  io.Writer
16: 	Verbose bool
17: }
18: 
19: // ToolEntry holds a tool's definition and executor functions.
20: type ToolEntry struct {
21: 	Definition func() provider.Tool
22: 	Execute    func(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) provider.ToolResult
23: }
24: 
25: // Registry maps tool names to their definitions and executors.
26: // Registration must happen at startup before any concurrent Dispatch calls.
27: // Concurrent Dispatch calls (read-only) are safe after registration is complete.
28: type Registry struct {
29: 	entries map[string]ToolEntry
30: }
31: 
32: // NewRegistry returns a new Registry with an empty, initialized entries map.
33: func NewRegistry() *Registry {
34: 	return &Registry{
35: 		entries: make(map[string]ToolEntry),
36: 	}
37: }
38: 
39: // Register adds a tool entry to the registry, keyed by name.
40: // If a tool with the same name already exists, it is silently replaced.
41: func (r *Registry) Register(name string, entry ToolEntry) {
42: 	r.entries[name] = entry
43: }
44: 
45: // Has returns true if a tool with the given name exists in the registry.
46: func (r *Registry) Has(name string) bool {
47: 	_, ok := r.entries[name]
48: 	return ok
49: }
50: 
51: // Resolve takes a list of tool names and returns their provider.Tool definitions.
52: // Each entry's Definition() is called on every Resolve invocation (not cached).
53: // Returns an error if any name is not found in the registry or has a nil Definition.
54: // Returns an empty non-nil slice for nil or empty input.
55: func (r *Registry) Resolve(names []string) ([]provider.Tool, error) {
56: 	if len(names) == 0 {
57: 		return []provider.Tool{}, nil
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	tools := make([]provider.Tool, 0, len(names))
61: 	for _, name := range names {
62: 		entry, ok := r.entries[name]
63: 		if !ok {
64: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", name)
65: 		}
66: 		if entry.Definition == nil {
67: 			return nil, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no definition", name)
68: 		}
69: 		tools = append(tools, entry.Definition())
70: 	}
71: 	return tools, nil
72: }
73: 
74: // Dispatch looks up the tool by call.Name and executes it.
75: // Returns (ToolResult, nil) on success, or (zero ToolResult, error) for
76: // unknown tools or tools with nil executors.
77: func (r *Registry) Dispatch(ctx context.Context, call provider.ToolCall, ec ExecContext) (provider.ToolResult, error) {
78: 	entry, ok := r.entries[call.Name]
79: 	if !ok {
80: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("unknown tool %q", call.Name)
81: 	}
82: 	if entry.Execute == nil {
83: 		return provider.ToolResult{}, fmt.Errorf("tool %q has no executor", call.Name)
84: 	}
85: 	return entry.Execute(ctx, call, ec), nil
86: }
87: 
88: // RegisterAll registers all implemented tool entries into the given registry.
89: // Both cmd/run.go and ExecuteCallAgent call this after NewRegistry().
90: // Future milestones add lines here — no call-site changes needed.
91: // Safe to call multiple times (Register silently replaces duplicates).
92: func RegisterAll(r *Registry) {
93: 	r.Register(toolname.ListDirectory, listDirectoryEntry())
94: 	r.Register(toolname.ReadFile, readFileEntry())
95: 	r.Register(toolname.WriteFile, writeFileEntry())
96: 	r.Register(toolname.EditFile, editFileEntry())
97: 	r.Register(toolname.RunCommand, runCommandEntry())

# Implementation Checklist: `url_fetch` Built-in Tool

**Spec:** `docs/plans/025_url_fetch_spec.md`
**GitHub Issue:** https://github.com/jrswab/axe/issues/7
**Created:** 2026-03-05

---

## Phase 1: Toolname Constant & Validation (no dependencies)

- [ ] Add `URLFetch = "url_fetch"` constant to `internal/toolname/toolname.go` const block (Req 1.1)
- [ ] Add `URLFetch: true` entry to the `ValidNames()` map in `internal/toolname/toolname.go` (Req 1.2)
- [ ] Update `TestValidNames_ReturnsExpectedCount` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: change expected count from 5 to 6 (Req 5.1)

- [ ] Update `TestConstants_Values` in `internal/toolname/toolname_test.go`: add table entry `{"URLFetch", URLFetch, "url_fetch"}` (Req 5.3)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify `internal/toolname` tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 2: Tool Tests (RED phase — write failing tests first)

- [ ] Create `internal/tool/url_fetch_test.go` with `package tool` declaration
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Success` — httptest server returns `"hello world"` status 200; verify `IsError` false, `Content` equals `"hello world"`, `CallID` matches (Req 3.12, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_EmptyURL` — empty `Arguments` map `{}`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"url is required"` (Req 3.3, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_MissingURLArgument` — `Arguments: {"url": ""}`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"url is required"` (Req 3.3, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_UnsupportedScheme_File` — `"file:///etc/passwd"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` and `"file"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_UnsupportedScheme_FTP` — `"ftp://example.com/file"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` and `"ftp"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_NoScheme` — `"example.com"`; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"unsupported scheme"` (Req 3.5, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxStatus_404` — httptest server returns `"not found"` status 404; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 404"` and `"not found"` (Req 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxStatus_500` — httptest server returns `"internal error"` status 500; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 500"` and `"internal error"` (Req 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_LargeResponseTruncation` — httptest server returns 20000 bytes of `'A'`; verify `IsError` false, `Content` contains `"[response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"`, `len(Content)` > 10000 but < 20000 (Req 3.10, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ExactLimitNotTruncated` — httptest server returns exactly 10000 bytes; verify `IsError` false, `Content` does NOT contain `"truncated"`, `len(Content)` equals 10000 (Req 3.10, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ContextCancellation` — httptest server blocks with `time.Sleep(10s)`, context timeout 50ms; verify `IsError` true (Req 3.7, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_ConnectionRefused` — URL to closed port on 127.0.0.1; verify `IsError` true, `Content` non-empty (Req 3.7, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_CallIDPassthrough` — httptest server returns 200, call.ID `"uf-unique-42"`; verify `CallID` equals `"uf-unique-42"` (Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_EmptyResponseBody` — httptest server returns 200 with empty body; verify `IsError` false, `Content` equals `""` (Req 3.12, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Write `TestURLFetch_Non2xxWithLargeBody` — httptest server returns 500 with > 10000 bytes; verify `IsError` true, `Content` contains `"HTTP 500"` and `"[response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` (Req 3.10, 3.11, Spec 7.2)
- [ ] Confirm all 15 tests compile but fail (RED state — `urlFetchEntry` does not exist yet)

## Phase 3: Tool Implementation (GREEN phase)

- [ ] Create `internal/tool/url_fetch.go` with `package tool` declaration (Req 2.1)
- [ ] Define package-level constant `maxReadBytes = 10000` (Req 3.9, Constraint 7)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchEntry() ToolEntry` returning `ToolEntry{Definition: urlFetchDefinition, Execute: urlFetchExecute}` (Req 2.2)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchDefinition() provider.Tool` with `Name: toolname.URLFetch`, description, and single `url` parameter (Type `"string"`, Required `true`) (Req 2.3, 2.4)
- [ ] Implement `urlFetchExecute(ctx, call, ec) provider.ToolResult` with deferred `toolVerboseLog` (Req 3.1, 3.13):
  - [ ] Extract `url` from `call.Arguments["url"]`; return error if empty (Req 3.2, 3.3)
  - [ ] Parse URL via `net/url.Parse`; return error on failure (Req 3.4)
  - [ ] Validate scheme is `"http"` or `"https"`; return `unsupported scheme %q: only http and https are allowed` on failure (Req 3.5)
  - [ ] Create request via `http.NewRequestWithContext(ctx, "GET", urlStr, nil)`; return error on failure (Req 3.6)
  - [ ] Execute via `http.DefaultClient.Do(req)`; return `err.Error()` on failure (Req 3.7)
  - [ ] Defer `resp.Body.Close()` (Req 3.8)
  - [ ] Read body via `io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(resp.Body, maxReadBytes+1))`; return error on failure (Req 3.9)
  - [ ] Truncate if `len(body) > maxReadBytes`: slice to `maxReadBytes`, append `"\n... [response truncated, exceeded 10000 characters]"` (Req 3.10)
  - [ ] If status < 200 or >= 300: return `ToolResult{IsError: true}` with `"HTTP %d: %s"` format (Req 3.11)

- [ ] Add `TestRegisterAll_RegistersURLFetch` to `internal/tool/registry_test.go`: verify `r.Has(toolname.URLFetch)` after `RegisterAll` (Spec 7.4)
- [ ] Add `TestRegisterAll_ResolvesURLFetch` to `internal/tool/registry_test.go`: verify resolved tool has `Name == toolname.URLFetch` and `url` parameter (Spec 7.4)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify registry tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 5: Agent Scaffold Update

- [ ] Update `Scaffold()` in `internal/agent/agent.go`: change tools comment from `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command` to `# Valid: list_directory, read_file, write_file, edit_file, run_command, url_fetch` (Req 6.1)
- [ ] Run `make test` — verify `internal/agent` tests pass and no regressions

## Phase 6: Full Validation

- [ ] Run `make test` — all tests pass with zero failures
- [ ] Verify `go build ./...` succeeds with no errors
- [ ] Verify `go.mod` is unchanged (no new dependencies, Constraint 1)
- [ ] Verify `cmd/run.go` is unchanged (Constraint 3)
- [ ] Verify `internal/tool/tool.go` is unchanged (Constraint 3)
- [ ] Verify `internal/provider/` is unchanged (Constraint 2)

# Skill: auto-review

Your job is to provide a comprehesive review of current code changes changes (`git diff HEAD`). This includes surfacing potential bugs, security issues, and code drift.

## Rules

- Always use the Context7 MCP for library/API documentation, code generation, setup, or configuration steps.

## Steps

1. Use `git diff HEAD` to get the changes.
2. Do a full code review on the diff:
    - Evaluate whether each change makes sense in the broader system.
    - Confirm the code does what it’s intended to do, especially for user-facing or critical paths.
    - Prioritize clear variable and function names, simple logic, and minimal complexity.
    - Ensure tests are meaningful, cover edge cases, and fail when the code breaks. Tests should be as maintainable as production code.
    - Avoid nitpicks that block progress unless they are critical. Use “nit” labels for minor style issues and mark non-blocking feedback clearly.
3. Your review output MUST be a markdown list and include:
    - A prompt for an LLM coding agent to fix the code. This prompt MUST be a specific and only address the fix needed.
    - Make sure to mention the file name prepend with `@` in the prompt and the line range.
